cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The task is to figure out which statement about Wu Jinyou is true based on the provided article. Let's start by identifying the relevant part of the text. The question is about Wu Jinyou, so I need to find the paragraph where he's mentioned. Scanning through the article, there's a section that says: "Wu Jinyou, head of the Polar Research Institute of China, and the group's leader, said, 'I hope the students have not only a field trip to the Yellow River station, but are also touched by the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers here. I also expect in the future, some of them will choose to devote themselves to polar research and maybe even work with us.'" From this, we can extract information about Wu Jinyou. The key points are: 1. He is the head of the Polar Research Institute of China. 2. He is the group's leader for this trip. 3. He expresses his hope that the students are inspired by the researchers' devotion and spirit. 4. He expects that some of the students might become polar researchers in the future. Now, let's look at the options provided: (A) He is a graduate from Dalian Marinetime University. - The article mentions Zheng Li as a student from Dalian Marinetime University but does not state that Wu Jinyou is a graduate from there. This is not supported. (B) He is the president of Dalian Marinetime University. - There's no mention of him being the president of any university, especially not Dalian Marinetime. This is false. (C) He thinks highly of the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers. - The quote from Wu Jinyou says he hopes the students are "touched by the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers." This implies he values their work and dedication, so this is likely true. (D) He is sure that all the ten students will make good polar researchers.
